channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Minneapolis grants consent for Blue Line Extension through city’s north side
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council signed off Wednesday on the proposed route of a light rail line that will run through a section of the city’s north side. However, some council members said they could only get to a yes vote after some of their concerns with the route were addressed.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Harris dodges answering whether athletes should stand during national anthem in unearthed interview
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris did not answer directly whether athletes should stand during the national anthem, an unearthed media interview in 2016 shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voters react to Vance 'non-answer' when asked if Trump won 2020 election
item.description: GOP vice presidential candidate JD Vance was questioned about former President Trump's prior statements on the 2020 elections – here's how voters reacted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Soomaalida St. Cloud waxay rabaan in ay ka dhex muuqdaan siyaasadda, laakiin iyaga oo dhan isku aragti ma aha
item.description: In ka badan labaatameeyo sano ka hor ayey qaxootigan oo ka soo jeeda Bariga Afrika soo dageen St. Cloud, oo qaarkood ayaa raadinaya in ay door ku yeeshaan maamulka iyo siyaasadda.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voters react to JD Vance claiming he and Donald Trump support 'clean air, clean water'
item.description: JD Vance said at the CBS Vice Presidential Debate that he and Donald Trump support "clean air, clean water," prompting mixed reactions from voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey Dem House candidate says she is 'not super worried' about biological men in women's locker rooms
item.description: A New Jersey Democrat and former women's basketball player running for Congress said she is "not super worried" about biological men being in women's locker rooms
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Helene renews calls for Congress to pass disaster funding
item.description: The widespread devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene is upping the pressure on lawmakers for a looming end-of-year fight over disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In South Texas congressional race, Monica De La Cruz and Michelle Vallejo spar over health care
item.description: The topic is playing a major role in one of Texas’ only competitive congressional races this year as the competitors debate Medicare, abortion and expanding coverage.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jasper Scherer, KPRC 2

item.title: Conservative social media reactions pour in declaring winner of VP debate: 'This was a massacre'
item.description: Social media reactions from conservatives came pouring in on social media following the first and only vice presidential debate on Tuesday night in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz and Vance go in depth on policy while attacking each other’s running mates in VP debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In a debate that evoked a calmer era in American politics, Tim Walz and JD Vance on Tuesday went after each other's running mates and sought to shore up their campaigns' vulnerabilities at a time of renewed fears of a regional war in the Middle East and sadness over devastation from
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, CHRIS MEGERIAN, WILL WEISSSERT and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance's debate answer on immigration crisis shows voter polarization in real time responses
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance received a mixed reaction from voters as he discussed the border crisis and potential deporations during his debate with Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some of the most notable quotes from the JD Vance-Tim Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The first and only vice presidential debate between Ohio Senator JD Vance and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz featured an often orderly, policy-focused and even civil dialogue between the two men seeking to serve as the next president's second in command.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What was the QR code on screen during the vice presidential debate?
item.description: CBS News is getting creative as it attempts to conduct Tuesday's Vice Presidential debate fairly and productively.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know on claims made about Minnesota, Walz in VP debate against Vance
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz’s home state of Minnesota came up early in his debate against fellow vice presidential candidate Ohio Sen. JD Vance on Tuesday evening. Here are the facts.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: A Virginia school board has agreed to pay a former high school teacher more than $500,000 in damages and attorneys' fees as part of a legal settlement after the instructor's dismissal in 2018 for refusing to use a transgender student's pronouns.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY race for US Senate: Gillibrand talks Adams indictment, term limits, economy
item.description: Come Nov. 5, the 15-year Democratic senator will defend her seat against Republican challenger Mike Sapraicone.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: The poll offers much better news for Trump than last month’s poll from Quinnipiac, which found Harris leading Trump by 3 points.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Texas Senate race toward Democrats
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted Texas’s Senate race from "likely Republican" to "lean Republican," signaling momentum for Rep. Colin Allred's (D) challenge against incumbent Sen. Ted Cruz (R) in the red state.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Should moderators fact-check? Veteran broadcast debate host, journalism professor weigh in
item.description: MPR News host emeritus Gary Eichten and University of Minnesota associate professor and director of the Minnesota Journalism Center Benjamin Toff joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer to talk about the positives and perils of live political debates.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: 5 possible October surprises that could roil the Trump-Harris race
item.description: Each of the past two presidential cycles have been marked by an October surprise. Here are five potential surprises that could emerge this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How Arizona's purple-shaded electorate factors into 2024 election
item.description: With Arizona's electorate more urban, the state has shifted more blue, turning the state purple in race predictability.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Walz, Vance split on abortion, border, economic policies in issues-driven debate
item.description: Questions around climate change, border concerns, abortion and the economy drove Tuesday night’s vice presidential faceoff. Vance and Walz remained largely cordial in the debate and steered away from personal attacks.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Harris and Trump change their campaign plans after Hurricane Helene's destruction
item.description: Hurricane Helene is scrambling the presidential candidates' plans this week. Democratic nominee Kamala Harris is cutting short a campaign visit to Las Vegas to return to Washington for briefings. Republican candidate Donald Trump is heading to Georgia to see the storm's impact.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, JILL COLVIN and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PIX11 News to host NY-18 debate with candidates Pat Ryan and Alison Esposito
item.description: September 30, 2024, New York, NY – PIX11 News today announced it will host a live debate for New York’s 18th Congressional District. Democratic incumbent Congressman Pat Ryan, a retired military intelligence officer in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PIX 11

item.title: Tim Walz’s experience in China could help him as veep, but he barely mentions it as a candidate
item.description: When Gov. Tim Walz first ran for Congress in 2006, his campaign proudly touted his extensive experience in China. In fact, earlier in his political career, he occasionally exaggerated it. But now that he’s running for vice president, Walz barely mentions China.
item.dc:creator: Forest Hunt, Caspar von Au, and Curtis Gilbert, MPR News

item.title: What Tim Walz's past debate performances reveal about how he may take on JD Vance
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz’s track record suggests the Democratic governor will focus on his experiences pre-politics. Those who’ve debated him before say Walz has a down-to-earth style that is relatable — but that style can also cause problems for the governor.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Walz attends Michigan-Minnesota college football game before final prep for Tuesday's debate
item.description: Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz has traveled to Ann Arbor, Michigan, to attend a football game between the University of Michigan and University of Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oregon senator introduces bill to reform Supreme Court, add 6 more seats
item.description: Oregon Sen. Ron Wyden introduced bill on Thursday that would bring fundamental changes to the nation's high court.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada's Horsford calls to 'end hate' following Republican congressman's tweet calling Haitians 'thugs'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Rep. Steven Horsford called for the censure of a Republican U.S. House member after that congressman posted and then deleted a racist tweet.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
